data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: UI elements are designed considering the time required to move to a target area on the screen.
data.technical_spec: Critical interactive components are prominently positioned for ease of access and action.

data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Reduces the user's decision-making time by limiting the number of choices presented.
data.technical_spec: Navigation menus structured with minimal options to prevent choice overload.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: A visually appealing design is believed to be more usable by users.
data.technical_spec: Clean, minimalist design with high-quality imagery and balanced color schemes.

data.title: Serial Position Effect
data.description: Important items are placed in strategic positions to enhance recall.
data.technical_spec: CTA and essential information prominently placed at strategic locations on a page.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites, so their experience is based on common design patterns.
data.technical_spec: Adhering to web conventions for navigation and layout to meet user expectations.
